definitions.candidate_set: For one checked finite certificate, U is the verified global upper endpoint and C is the set of canonical orders sigma with verified strict lower endpoint L_sigma <= U.
definitions.excluded_order: An excluded order has verified strict lower endpoint L_sigma > U.
definitions.exclusion_gap: When excluded orders exist, the exclusion gap is min(L_sigma - U) over excluded orders; otherwise it is null.
definitions.identical_serialized_brackets: Groups of canonical orders whose source summary lower and upper endpoint strings are identical.

candidate_set_membership.basis: verified source lower endpoints compared with verified global upper endpoint

excluded_order_count.basis: verified source lower endpoints compared with verified global upper endpoint

exclusion_gap.basis: verified source lower endpoints minus verified global upper endpoint

identical_serialized_bracket_groups.basis: string identity in semantically validated source certificate summaries

cross_n_trends.basis: patterns visible only in the listed checked finite inputs

limitations.basis: explicit scope boundaries not settled by this artifact

candidate_set.semantics: canonical orders whose verified strict lower endpoint L_sigma is <= verified global upper endpoint U
candidate_set.warning: The unique candidate is modulo the current rotation/reflection convention.

excluded_orders.semantics: canonical orders whose verified strict lower endpoint L_sigma is > verified global upper endpoint U

identical_serialized_bracket_groups.warning: Identical serialized bracket endpoints are not an exact equality claim for fixed-order optima.

exploratory_asymptotic_ratios.warning: These finite-n diagnostics are numerical observations only.

limitations: Finite checked certificate evidence for this n only.
Candidate multiplicity is not an exact tie claim.
Identical serialized brackets are not exact optimum equality claims.

commands.output_summary: Built derived finite-results summary from 4 semantically validated source certificate artifact(s).

limitations: Derived from checked finite certificates for n=3,4,5,6 only.
Does not establish exact optimum values.
Does not establish exact ties between multiple candidate orders.
Ratios and cross-n sequences do not establish behavior beyond the listed inputs.
Depends on the documented guarded mpmath.iv interval-backend contract of the source certificates.
